content:
# Foam Stability

**Summary**: The ability of a foam to maintain its structure and integrity under specific conditions.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2019-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2019]] evaluated foam-stability as the primary factor for successful CO2 EOR.

- [[2024-gowida|Gowida, 2024]] evaluated foam stability as a function of pressure, temperature, and additives.

- [[2024-al-drwish|AL-DRWISH, 2024]] evaluated foam stability as the primary parameter for determining the success of acid fracturing treatments.

## Scientific Context

- [[2019-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2019]] Critical for ensuring that foam can effectively reduce gas mobility.

- [[2024-gowida|Gowida, 2024]] evaluated foam stability as a function of pressure, temperature, and additives.

- [[2024-al-drwish|AL-DRWISH, 2024]] evaluated foam stability as the primary parameter for determining the success of acid fracturing treatments.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2019-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2019]] Monitored via pressure drop across the core.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- N2 addition improved foam stability at supercritical conditions where pure CO2 was weak. ([[2019-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2019, p. 18]])


- At 1000 psi, the foam half-life increased from 97 minutes at 14.7 psi to over 350 minutes. ([[2024-gowida|Gowida, 2024, p. 63]])

- N2-foam was more stable than CO2-foam because the higher permeability of CO2 through the film led to faster bubble coalescence. ([[2024-al-drwish|AL-DRWISH, 2024, p. 54]])
